content.cn_text: 你将听到两名健康科学专业的学生 Elliot 和 Beth 讨论关于气候
content.en_text: You will hear two health science students called Elliot and Beth discussing research on climate

content.cn_text: 变化和过敏的研究。首先，你有一些时间浏览第21至第25题。
content.en_text: change and allergies. First you have some time to look at questions 21 to 25.

content.cn_text: 现在请仔细听，并回答第21至第25题。 Elliot：你有机会看过
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 21 to 25. Elliot: Have you had a chance to look at the

content.cn_text: 我们项目的信息了吗，Beth？ Beth：我已经看了大部分关于
content.en_text: information for our project yet, Beth? Beth: I've had a look at most of the stuff on the relationship

content.cn_text: 气候变化和过敏之间关系的资料，以及开花植物和树木花粉所起的作用。
content.en_text: between climate change and allergies and the role played by pollen from flowering plants and trees.

content.cn_text: Elliot：我也看了。主要信息是季节性过敏似乎正在恶化。仅仅在
content.en_text: Elliot: Yeah, me too. The main message is that seasonal allergies seem to be getting worse. In just

content.cn_text: 过去20年里，桦树花朵产生的花粉量已经增加到三倍，
content.en_text: the last 20 years the amount of pollen that's produced by the flowers on birch trees has tripled

content.cn_text: 四分之一的人患有花粉引发的花粉症或哮喘。
content.en_text: and one in four people suffers from either hay fever or asthma which is triggered by pollen.

content.cn_text: Beth：我读到过一些内容，说过敏患者更可能有维生素D缺乏，
content.en_text: Beth: I read something about allergy sufferers being more likely to have a vitamin D deficiency

content.cn_text: 因为我们不再像20世纪50年代英国许多人那样服用鱼肝油作为维生素补充剂。
content.en_text: because we no longer take cod liver oil as a vitamin supplement like many people used to do in

content.cn_text: 那是在20世纪50年代的英国，而从那以后，大多数欧洲国家的过敏病例增加到四倍。
content.en_text: the 1950s in Britain and since then allergies have quadrupled in most European countries

content.cn_text: 但鱼肝油这么受欢迎只是在英国，所以这个论点其实
content.en_text: but it was only in Britain that cod liver oil was so popular so that argument doesn't really

content.cn_text: 说不通。 Elliot：是的，似乎不太相关。
content.en_text: make sense. Elliot: No, it doesn't seem that relevant.

content.cn_text: Beth：现在似乎发生的是，越来越多人在四五十岁时首次发作过敏。
content.en_text: Beth: What seems to be happening now is that an increasing number of people are getting their first attack in their 40s and 50s.

content.cn_text: Elliot：那一定是因为气候变化导致气温升高，
content.en_text: Elliot: That must be because of the link with climate change causing rising temperatures

content.cn_text: 但二氧化碳水平也在严重影响花粉数量，Beth：影响程度和更温和的冬季一样。
content.en_text: but CO2 levels are seriously affecting pollen counts too. Beth：As much as milder winters.

content.cn_text: Elliot: 确实，二氧化碳有助于刺激植物生长，最近几项研究表明，当
content.en_text: Elliot: Definitely, CO2 helps stimulate plant growth and several recent studies have shown that when

content.cn_text: 植物暴露在更多二氧化碳中时，它们会产生更多花粉。
content.en_text: plants are exposed to more CO2 they produce more pollen.

content.cn_text: Beth：真的吗？所以未来人们可能会经历更严重的过敏症状？ Elliot: 没错。
content.en_text: Beth：Really? So people might experience more severe allergic symptoms in future? Elliot: That's right.

content.cn_text: Beth：另一个因素是花粉季正在改变。 Elliot：是的，科学家预测到2130年春天会提前五周到来。
content.en_text: Beth：And another factor is that the pollen season is changing. Elliot: Yes, scientists are predicting spring will come five weeks earlier by 2130.

content.cn_text: Beth：那是长期趋势，但这并不意味着每年春天都会更早到来。看看
content.en_text: Beth: That's in the long term but that doesn't mean that spring will come earlier every year. Look at

content.cn_text: 去年冬天一直冷到三月，花粉季推迟了，而今年
content.en_text: last winter that was really cold until March and the pollen season was delayed whereas this year

content.cn_text: 春天来得更早，而且持续得更久。
content.en_text: spring came earlier and lasted longer.

content.cn_text: Elliot：对，另一个我们应该提到的是豚草。 Beth：它是植物物种因气候变化进入新区域的例子。
content.en_text: Elliot: Right and another thing we should mention is ragweed. Beth: As an example of a plant species moving into new areas as a result of climate change.

content.cn_text: Elliot：是的，在美国，它已经是八月和九月影响数百万人的主要过敏原，
content.en_text: Elliot: Yes, in the US it's already the major allergen affecting millions of people in August and

content.cn_text: 但我们现在开始看到它首次在欧洲生长，这真的令人担忧，
content.en_text: September but we're starting to see it grow in Europe for the first time which is really worrying

content.cn_text: 因为它非常容易引发过敏。
content.en_text: because it's so allergenic.

content.cn_text: Beth：树木花粉在城市中也更严重，因为那里有很多硬质表面，
content.en_text: Beth: The problem with tree pollen is also worse in cities because there are so many hard surfaces

content.cn_text: 花粉不太会被吸收，而是停留在空气中。
content.en_text: around and the pollen doesn't get absorbed so much, just stays in the air.

content.cn_text: Elliot：是的，不是有研究显示某个城市的花粉量高三倍吗？Beth: 我记得是。
content.en_text: Elliot: Yeah, wasn't the pollen shown to be three times higher in one city? Beth: I think so.

content.cn_text: 因此，许多城市已经开始禁止种植某些类型的树。
content.en_text: So because of this many cities have started to ban the planting of certain types of trees.

content.cn_text: 在听剩余讨论之前，你有一些时间浏览第26至第30题。
content.en_text: Before you hear the rest of the discussion you have some time to look at questions 26 to 30.

content.cn_text: 现在请听录音，并回答第26至第30题。
content.en_text: Now listen and answer questions 26 to 30.

content.cn_text: Beth：那我们来看看欧洲花粉季节变化的不同数据表，
content.en_text: Beth: So shall we look at the different data tables for seasonal changes

content.cn_text: 好吗？ Elliot：可以，但我觉得我们应该只关注那些我们有最多数据的树木和植物。
content.en_text: for pollen in Europe? Elliot: Okay, but I think we should just focus on the trees and plants which we have

content.cn_text: 也就是橡树、桦树和草。 Beth：是的，这样更容易处理。
content.en_text: the most data for. So that's oak trees and birch trees and grass. Beth: Yes, that makes it more manageable.

content.cn_text: 我们要不要详细查看每个国家的数据，并挑出有意思的地方？ Elliot：好，从奥地利开始。
content.en_text: Shall we go through the data for each country in detail and pick out anything interesting? Elliot: All right, so starting with Austria.

content.cn_text: 这里我们只有桦树花粉的数据。
content.en_text: Well, here we've only got data for birch pollen.

content.cn_text: 我想知道为什么没有橡树或草的数据。 Beth：嗯，和邻国比较会很有意思。
content.en_text: I wonder why there's no data for oak or grass. Beth: Hmm, it would be interesting to compare with neighbouring countries.

content.cn_text: Elliot：确实。Beth: 法国的情况很不寻常。 Elliot: 是的，橡树和桦树的花粉季都开始得更早，
content.en_text: Elliot: True. Beth: The picture in France is unusual. Elliot: Yes, the pollen season starts earlier for both oak and birch,

content.cn_text: 但草的花粉季没有更早开始。我想知道为什么。
content.en_text: but it doesn't start any earlier for grass. I wonder why that is.

content.cn_text: 在荷兰，我们有三种植物的完整数据。 Beth：那数据显示什么？
content.en_text: In the Netherlands, we've got complete data for all three plants. Beth: So what does it show?

content.cn_text: 草和桦树花粉只提前了几天，但橡树花粉开始时间提前得多，
content.en_text: Grass and birch pollen are only starting a few days earlier, but oak pollen is starting much

content.cn_text: 实际上提前了18天。 嗯，这很不寻常。其他国家没有这么大的差异。
content.en_text: earlier, 18 days in fact. Hmm, that's unusual. There's not such a big difference in other countries.

content.cn_text: Elliot：是的，瑞士是个有趣的案例。这里花粉季比过去开始得更早，
content.en_text: Elliot: No, Switzerland's an interesting case. The pollen season starts here earlier than in the past for

content.cn_text: 包括橡树、桦树和草。 Beth：是的，提前幅度从21天到14天不等。
content.en_text: oak, birch and grass. Beth: Yeah, from 21 days to 14 days earlier.

content.cn_text: Elliot：是的，英国又显示出不同情况。Beth: 这里的问题是潮湿的夏季把草花粉季从五周延长到
content.en_text: Elliot: Yeah, the UK shows a different picture again. Beth: Yeah, here the problem is that wet summers have extended the grass pollen season from five

content.cn_text: 八周，这对过敏患者来说是坏消息。 Elliot：对。嗯，我觉得我们应该……
content.en_text: weeks to eight weeks, which is bad news for allergy sufferers. Elliot：Right. Well, I think we should have...

content.cn_text: 第三部分到此结束。你现在有30秒时间检查第三部分的答案。
content.en_text: That is the end of part three. You now have 30 seconds to check your answers to part three.

groups.desc: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.
groups.title: Questions 21 – 25

items.title: Why is Beth unconvinced that allergies are linked to vitamin D deficiency?

options.title: A
options.content: because people’s diets have improved since the 1950s

options.title: B
options.content: because most people get enough vitamin D

options.title: C
options.content: because the evidence only applies in Britain

items.analysis: 答案解析：第21题问 Beth 为什么不相信过敏和维生素D缺乏有关。原文说鱼肝油流行只发生在英国，但过敏增加出现在多数欧洲国家，所以证据只适用于英国，答案是 C. because the evidence only applies in Britain。

items.title: Eliot explains that rising CO2 levels

options.title: A
options.content: will mean distribution of pollen over a wider area.

options.title: B
options.content: are having less impact on pollen than milder winters.

options.title: C
options.content: are responsible for higher quantities of pollen.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第22题问 Elliot 解释二氧化碳水平上升会怎样。原文说 CO2 affects pollen counts，且植物暴露在更多 CO2 下会 produce more pollen，所以答案是 C. are responsible for higher quantities of pollen。

items.title: What does Beth say about seasonal changes?

options.title: A
options.content: There is no clear short-term trend for the arrival of spring.

options.title: B
options.content: In general, spring is likely to continue arriving early.

options.title: C
options.content: Springs are becoming milder and longer.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第23题问 Beth 对季节变化说了什么。原文说长期来看春天会提前，但并不意味着每年都提前，并举去年推迟、今年提前的例子，所以答案是 A. There is no clear short-term trend for the arrival of spring。

items.title: What point is made about the plant ragweed?

options.title: A
options.content: It is spreading to more countries.

options.title: B
options.content: It is the most widespread cause of allergies globally.

options.title: C
options.content: It causes the most severe type of allergy.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第24题问关于 ragweed 提到了什么。原文说它作为因气候变化进入新区域的植物例子，并开始在欧洲生长，所以答案是 A. It is spreading to more countries。

items.title: Why is tree pollen a more serious problem in cities?

options.title: B
options.content: There is less to absorb the tree pollen.

options.title: C
options.content: There is not enough control over tree-planting.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第25题问为什么树木花粉在城市中问题更严重。原文说城市硬质表面多，花粉不容易被吸收，只会停留在空气中，所以答案是 B. There is less to absorb the tree pollen。

groups.desc: Choose FIVE answers from the box and write the correct letter, A–G, next to questions 26–30.
groups.title: Questions 26 – 30

list.title: A
list.content: greatest change recorded for oak pollen

list.title: B
list.content: changes identified for two pollen types only

list.title: C
list.content: a longer season for grass pollen

list.title: D
list.content: earlier start dates for all pollen types

list.title: E
list.content: significant change in start date of birch pollen

list.title: G
list.content: information only available for birch pollen

items.title: Austria
items.analysis: 答案解析：第26题问 Austria 的花粉季节变化评论。原文说 only got data for birch pollen，没有 oak 或 grass 数据，所以答案是 G. information only available for birch pollen。

items.title: France
items.analysis: 答案解析：第27题问 France 的评论。原文说 oak and birch 两种花粉季提前，但 grass 没有提前，所以答案是 B. changes identified for two pollen types only。

items.title: The Netherlands
items.analysis: 答案解析：第28题问 The Netherlands 的评论。原文说 oak pollen 提前18天，而草和桦树只提前几天，是橡树变化最大，所以答案是 A. greatest change recorded for oak pollen。

items.title: Switzerland
items.analysis: 答案解析：第29题问 Switzerland 的评论。原文说 oak, birch and grass 的花粉季都比过去开始更早，所以答案是 D. earlier start dates for all pollen types。

items.title: UK
items.analysis: 答案解析：第30题问 UK 的评论。原文说 grass pollen season 从五周延长到八周，所以答案是 C. a longer season for grass pollen。
